title: The Negro Speaks of Rivers Summary In Hindi | Line By Line Explanation In Hindi
description:
Langston Hughes wrote "The Negro Speaks of Rivers" at just seventeen, scribbling it on an envelope while crossing the Mississippi — a river that had carried enslaved Black people to auction blocks — and that biographical fact alone tells you why this poem carries the weight it does. Rooted in themes of racial identity and collective memory, this Hughes poem traces Black civilization across the Euphrates, Congo, Nile, and Mississippi, making it essential reading for BA English Honours students and those exploring world poetry in their curriculum. If you need The Negro Speaks of Rivers summary in Hindi with line by line explanation, this is exactly where Hughes's ancient, deep-flowing pride finally speaks your language.

thenegrospeaksofrivers

"The Negro Speaks of Rivers" is a poem by American writer Langston Hughes. Hughes wrote the poem when he was seventeen and crossing the Mississippi River on the way to visit his father in Mexico. It was first published the following year in The Crisis, starting Hughes's literary career.
